rule.statement: Default to Vitest; pick Jest only when a Jest-only tool blocks migration.

rationale.source: Vitest docs; Vitest vs Jest benchmark

rule.rationale: Vitest is faster, ESM-native, Jest-API-compatible; Jest carries legacy ESM friction.

rule.statement: DOM-level tests use Testing Library; enzyme / direct ReactDOM is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Testing Library forces accessibility-friendly queries; enzyme tied to React internals.

rule.statement: HTTP mocking goes through MSW (request interception at network level), never Jest's `fetch` spy.
rule.rationale: MSW handlers are reusable across unit / integration / e2e; spies are per-test boilerplate.

rule.statement: Coverage runs with V8 provider + branch coverage; same thresholds as code-coverage methodology.
rule.rationale: Without branch coverage the gate misses untested else-branches.

rule.statement: A test that only asserts via toMatchSnapshot() is rejected; every test has ≥1 behavioural assertion alongside any snapshot.
rule.rationale: Snapshot-only tests turn into rubber stamps; behavioural assertions stay legible.
